response:
# Perspective Audit — Search Results With No Live Region Announcements ## Step 1 — Escalated perspectives confirmed From the a11y-critic escalation: - **Screen Reader & Semantic Structure — HIGH** → deep review - **Cognitive & Neurodivergent — MEDIUM** → deep review Not escalated (LOW / not reviewed): Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion, Auditory Access, Keyboard & Motor. **Scope note (Vestibular & Motion):** the `.spinner` keyframe animation at CSS lines 150–162 has no `prefers-reduced-motion` guard. That is a Vestibular & Motion concern, and that perspective was **not** escalated, so I am not opening its checklist here (evidence over assertion — only fire where the signal exists). Flagging it as a one-line pointer for whoever owns the motion pass, not as a finding in this audit. --- ## Step 4 — Per-perspective checklists ### Perspective 6 — Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (HIGH) | # | Checklist item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | Landmark regions present/correct (1.3.1) | **PASS** | `
` at JSX line 46; results `
` becomes a labeled region at line 68 | | 2 | Heading hierarchy logical, `

` present (2.4.6) | **PASS** | `

` line 47; each result `

` line 79 — h1→h2, no skipped levels | | 3 | Informative images have `alt`; decorative `alt=""` (1.1.1) | **N/A** | No `` elements; the `×` is text content, not an image | | 4 | DOM order matches visual reading order (1.3.2) | **PASS** | Source order: h1 → search bar → count → spinner → results → no-results; matches visual layout | | 5 | Unique descriptive `` (2.4.2) | **HUMAN VERIFY** | Component renders no document `<title>`; route-level title management not visible in source | | 6 | Link text describes destination (2.4.4) | **PASS** | Links use article titles, e.g. "Getting Started with React" (line 79) — descriptive in context | | 7 | `lang` on `<html>` (3.1.1) | **HUMAN VERIFY** | Component does not render `<html>`; document-level attribute out of source scope | | 8 | Foreign-language phrases wrapped in `lang` (3.1.2) | **N/A** | No foreign-language content | | 9 | Every input has associated visible `<label>` (3.3.2) | **PASS** | `<label htmlFor="search-input">` line 51 ↔ `<input id="search-input">` line 54 | | 10 | Error messages field-identified + `aria-describedby` (3.3.1) | **N/A** | No form-validation errors; search filters as-you-type | | 11 | Custom widgets use correct ARIA roles (4.1.2) | **FINDING** | Clear `<button>` line 62 has correct implicit role but **no accessible name** — see Finding 3 | | 12 | Status messages use `aria-live` (4.1.3) | **FINDING** | Results count / result set / loading all update silently — see Findings 1 and 2 | | 13 | SPA route-change title/focus/announce | **N/A (route)** / **FINDING (announce)** | This is in-page filtering, not a route change; the "announce" dimension is the 4.1.3 gap in Finding 1 | **Semantic sub-item — input type:** `type="text"` at line 55 exposes `role="textbox"` where a search field should expose `role="searchbox"` — see Finding 4. **Red-flag scan (auto-CRITICAL triggers):** none fire. - No `<main>` → `<main>` **is** present. No. - Input without any label → input **is** labeled. No. - Custom widget with no ARIA role → clear button is a **native** `<button>` with an implicit role; the defect is a missing *name*, not a missing *role*. The red flag as worded does **not** fire, so this is **not** auto-CRITICAL. (Calling this out explicitly so severity is driven by user impact, not by a checklist reflex.) - Missing `lang` on `<html>` → not renderable from component source; flagged for human verification, not asserted as a violation. --- ### Perspective 7 — Cognitive & Neurodivergent (MEDIUM) | # | Checklist item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | Error messages suggest corrective action (3.3.3) | **PASS** | "No results found. Try different keywords." (line 85) is specific and actionable | | 2 | Multi-step form doesn't re-ask prior data (3.3.7) | **N/A** | Single-field search, not a multi-step form | | 3 | Auth allows paste/autofill, no puzzles (3.3.8) | **N/A** | No authentication | | 4 | Navigation same relative position across pages (3.2.3) | **HUMAN VERIFY** | Single component; site chrome not in scope | | 5 | Same-function components consistently labeled (3.2.4) | **HUMAN VERIFY** | Cannot compare across pages from one component | | 6 | Help mechanism in consistent location (3.2.6) | **N/A** | No help mechanism in component | | 7 | Focus does not auto-trigger context change (3.2.1) | **PASS** | No `onFocus` handlers; focusing input/results changes nothing | | 8 | Changing control value doesn't auto-submit/navigate (3.2.2) | **PASS** | Typing filters the results list in place — no focus move, no navigation, no submit. Live-filtering a results region is not a "change of context" under WCAG. See observation note below on the *silent* dimension | | 9 | Destructive actions have undo/confirm (3.3.4) | **PASS (N/A)** | Clear button wipes only the query; trivially reversible by retyping. 3.3.4 scope is legal/financial/data transactions — not applicable | | 10 | More than one way to reach a page (2.4.5) | **HUMAN VERIFY** | This search *is* one of the ways; sitewide paths not assessable here | | 11 | Personal-data inputs have `autocomplete` (1.3.5) | **N/A** | Search query is not a 1.3.5 input-purpose field (name/email/tel/etc.) | | 12 | Timeout warnings ≥ 20s (2.2.1) | **N/A** | No timeouts | | 13 | Avoids unexplained jargon / complex sentences | **PASS (UI) / HUMAN VERIFY (content)** | UI chrome ("Search documentation", "Enter a search term") is plain. Result copy contains "RSC", "memoize", "reducers" — audience-appropriate editorial content for dev docs; reading level is a content-author call | **Neurodivergent-specific checks:** - Predictable layout / no unexpected shift — **PARTIAL.** Content in expected location, but the results region rebuilds as the user types (spinner ↔ results ↔ no-results). This is a conventional search-as-you-type pattern and does not move focus, but the transition is conveyed only visually and only by a spinning glyph. See observation note. - Clear visual hierarchy — **PASS.** h1/h2 structure and spacing guide reading order. - No autoplay media / unprompted modals — **PASS.** - Instructions don't rely on memory of prior steps — **PASS.** Single-step task. **Red-flag scan (auto-CRITICAL triggers):** none fire. No CAPTCHA/cognitive test, no paste-blocked field, no redundant re-entry, no unconfirmed destructive action. **Honest calibration for Perspective 7:** at the source-verifiable level, this perspective **passes** its in-scope checklist. It earned a MEDIUM escalation because the primary defects — results that update with **no perceivable completion cue** and an **icon-only control with no name** — also raise cognitive load for neurodivergent users, not only screen-reader users. Those two barriers are already captured as MAJOR findings under Perspective 6 (Findings 1 and 3), and **their fixes resolve the cognitive impact too** (a `role="status"` "Searching…/N results" cue is a visible, textual, persistent confirmation that helps attention/processing differences as much as it helps AT). I am **not** manufacturing duplicate findings with weaker citations to "justify" the MEDIUM rating — a MEDIUM escalation is a prompt to run the checklist, not a guarantee of independent findings. The genuine net-new cognitive item is the loading-state cue (Finding 2), which is a shared fix. --- ## Step 5 — Findings ### Finding 1 — Search results change with no live-region announcement **Finding:** As the user types, the results count, the result set, and the empty state all update, but nothing is announced to assistive technology. A screen-reader user receives no feedback that a search ran, how many results were returned, or that zero results matched. The results `<section>` carries `aria-label="Search results"` (helps *find* the region) but has no `aria-live`, `role="status"`, or `aria-busy` (nothing announces that it *changed*). **WCAG:** 4.1.3 Status Messages (AA) **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (also degrades Cognitive & Neurodivergent) **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author for announcement wording) **Evidence:** JSX lines 68–72 — `<section className="results-section" aria-label="Search results">` wrapping `<p className="results-count">{query ? \`${results.length} results for "${query}"\` : 'Enter a search term'}</p>` with no live-region attribute anywhere in the section. **Fix:** Wrap the count/status text in a container exposed as a live region, e.g. `<p className="results-count" role="status" aria-live="polite">…</p>`, so each settled result count is announced politely without moving focus. Announce the empty state through the same region. **Severity rationale (not CRITICAL):** the results remain perceivable — a screen-reader user can browse into the labeled "Search results" region and read the count and cards manually. Access is not *blocked*; it is a significant, unassisted barrier requiring the user to poll for changes. That is MAJOR, not CRITICAL. (CRITICAL is reserved for total blocks — keyboard trap, captionless speech video, alternativeless CAPTCHA.) ### Finding 2 — Loading state is not programmatically exposed **Finding:** The loading indicator is a CSS-only spinning `<div>` with no text, `role`, or `aria-busy`. During the debounce/fetch window a screen-reader user (and any user who does not interpret a spinning glyph) gets no "Searching…" signal. Because the results announcement in Finding 1 is also absent, the entire load→result transition is silent. **WCAG:** 4.1.3 Status Messages (AA) **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (shared fix serves Cognitive & Neurodivergent) **Severity:** MINOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author for "Searching…" text) **Evidence:** JSX line 75 — `{loading && <div className="spinner" />}`; CSS lines 150–158 define a purely visual `.spinner`. No `aria-busy` on the results section, no visually-hidden text. **Fix:** Give the spinner an accessible status, e.g. `<div className="spinner" role="status"><span className="sr-only">Searching…</span></div>`, and/or set `aria-busy={loading}` on the results section. Provide an `.sr-only` utility if one does not exist. **Severity rationale (MINOR):** the loading window is a short transient (300 ms debounce) and the substantive gap is the *results* announcement in Finding 1; once that live region exists, a missed transient "Searching…" degrades but does not block the experience. ### Finding 3 — Clear button has no accessible name **Finding:** The clear control is a native `<button>` whose only content is the character `×` (U+00D7 MULTIPLICATION SIGN). Screen readers announce this as "times" / "multiplication sign" (or nothing meaningful), never "clear search." A screen-reader or voice-control user cannot tell what the button does, and voice control has no reliable spoken label to target. **WCAG:** 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (A) **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (also raises Cognitive load — icon-only affordance) **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author for label wording) **Evidence:** JSX line 62 — `<button className="clear-btn" onClick={() => setQuery('')}>×</button>`. Accessible name computes to "×"; no `aria-label`, `aria-labelledby`, or visually-hidden text. **Fix:** Add `aria-label="Clear search"` to the button and mark the glyph decorative: `<button className="clear-btn" aria-label="Clear search" onClick={…}><span aria-hidden="true">×</span></button>`. **Severity rationale (MAJOR not CRITICAL):** a workaround exists — the user can clear the field by selecting its text and deleting — and search itself still functions. The specific control is unusable to AT/voice users, which is a significant barrier: MAJOR. ### Finding 4 — Search input uses `type="text"` instead of `type="search"` **Finding:** The search field is declared `type="text"`, exposing `role="textbox"` where a documentation search field should expose the `searchbox` role. Assistive technology announces a generic edit field rather than a search field, so the input's purpose is under-communicated programmatically. **WCAG:** 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (A) **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** MINOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev **Evidence:** JSX line 55 — `type="text"` inside the labeled search input (lines 53–59); confirmed by the source comment at line 50. **Fix:** Change to `type="search"`. Low effort; also enables native UA affordances. **Severity rationale (MINOR):** the field is labeled and fully operable either way; the role refinement improves programmatic clarity but removes no barrier to completing the task. Honest call: MINOR, bordering ENHANCEMENT — not inflated despite being a planted "semantic bug." --- ## A11y Evidence Finding blocks (MAJOR findings — contract-compatible) ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: search-results-no-live-region fingerprint: b3f19a2c7d40e85f source: perspective-audit Screen Reader & Semantic checklist (item 12, 4.1.3) wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 SC 4.1.3 Status Messages (AA) section_508_fpc_context: Not in Revised Section 508 scope — 4.1.3 is a WCAG 2.1 addition; Revised 508 references WCAG 2.0 A/AA. Equivalent barrier is nonetheless real for E205 content. severity: MAJOR perspective_alarms: screen_reader_semantic=HIGH, cognitive_neurodivergent=MEDIUM evidence: SearchPage JSX lines 68–72 — results `<section aria-label="Search results">` and `<p className="results-count">` update on each query change with no aria-live/role=status/aria-busy. reproduction_steps: source review only — Tab to search input, type a query with a screen reader running; observe no announcement of loading, result count, or empty state. expected_behavior: Result count and empty state announced politely (aria-live="polite" / role="status") without moving focus; loading optionally announced. actual_behavior: Results, count, and no-results state change silently; AT user must manually browse the region to discover any change. trend: persistent Route to: Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author) ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: clear-button-no-accessible-name fingerprint: a1c8e0f4529d6b73 source: perspective-audit Screen Reader & Semantic checklist (item 11, 4.1.2) wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 SC 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (A) section_508_fpc_context: In scope — maps to Revised Section 508 / WCAG 2.0 SC 4.1.2 (A). severity: MAJOR perspective_alarms: screen_reader_semantic=HIGH, cognitive_neurodivergent=MEDIUM evidence: SearchPage JSX line 62 — `<button className="clear-btn" onClick={() => setQuery('')}>×</button>`; accessible name computes to "×" (U+00D7), no aria-label/aria-labelledby. reproduction_steps: source review only — with a screen reader, Tab to the clear control after entering a query; observe announcement "times, button" (or equivalent) with no purpose. expected_behavior: Control announced as "Clear search, button"; glyph marked decorative. actual_behavior: Control announced as the multiplication-sign character; purpose not conveyed to AT or voice control. trend: persistent Route to: Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author) --- ## Step 6 — Perspective Audit Summary **Component/artifact reviewed:** `SearchPage` — documentation search with as-you-type results (React component + CSS) **Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (HIGH); Cognitive & Neurodivergent (MEDIUM) **Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow; Environmental Contrast; Vestibular & Motion; Auditory Access; Keyboard & Motor ### Findings by Severity | Severity | Count | |---|---| | CRITICAL | 0 | | MAJOR | 2 | | MINOR | 2 | | ENHANCEMENT | 0 | ### Perspectives That Passed - **Cognitive & Neurodivergent — PASS at source level.** All source-verifiable checklist items pass or are N/A; the no-results message is a genuine strength (specific corrective action, 3.3.3). No net-new code-level finding beyond the shared 4.1.3/4.1.2 defects already routed under Screen Reader & Semantic. Cross-page items (3.2.3, 3.2.4, 3.2.6, 2.4.5) and result-copy reading level remain for human verification. - **Screen Reader & Semantic — did not fully pass** (2 MAJOR + 2 MINOR). Strong foundation, though: correct `<main>`/`<section>` landmarks, clean h1→h2 outline, programmatically associated label, descriptive link text, DOM order matching visual order. ### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role **Front-End Dev** - No live region for results changes — Screen Reader & Semantic — MAJOR — 4.1.3 - Clear button missing accessible name — Screen Reader & Semantic — MAJOR — 4.1.2 - Loading state not programmatically exposed — Screen Reader & Semantic — MINOR — 4.1.3 - Search input `type="text"` not `type="search"` — Screen Reader & Semantic — MINOR — 1.3.1 **Content Author** (secondary) - Announcement wording for the results status region ("N results for …", "No results found") — supports Finding 1 - Label wording for the clear control ("Clear search") — supports Finding 3 **UX Design** (secondary) - Define the intended status-announcement pattern (what is announced, at what politeness, on load vs. settle) — supports Findings 1 and 2 **Visual Design** - None in scope for the escalated perspectives. ### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification - Document `<title>` uniqueness/descriptiveness (2.4.2) — not renderable from component source. *[Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]* - `<html lang>` presence and correctness (3.1.1) — document-level, outside component. *[Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]* - Navigation position consistency (3.2.3), consistent identification of repeated controls (3.2.4), help-location consistency (3.2.6), and multiple ways to reach pages (2.4.5) — require multi-page context. *[Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]* - Result description reading level / undefined abbreviations ("RSC", "memoize") for the target dev-docs audience (Cognitive, content-level). *[Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]* - Out-of-scope pointer: `.spinner` animation has no `prefers-reduced-motion` guard (CSS 150–162) — belongs to the **Vestibular & Motion** perspective, which was not escalated; route there if a motion pass is opened. ### Recommended Next Step **REVISE** — Two MAJOR findings (silent results updates, unnamed clear button) must be addressed before shipping; no CRITICAL findings block outright. Both MAJOR fixes are small, well-understood changes (`role="status"`/`aria-live` on the results status text; `aria-label` on the clear button) that also resolve the Cognitive & Neurodivergent concerns that drove the MEDIUM escalation. Fold in the two MINOR fixes (`aria-busy`/status text for loading; `type="search"`) in the same pass, then re-test with a11y-test (axe scan + real screen-reader/keyboard walkthrough of the type→load→result→clear cycle).

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
